data.content:
### Create IAM Role for EC2 Bedrock Access (AWS CLI)

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/bedrock.md

These AWS CLI commands demonstrate the process of creating an IAM role (`EC2-Bedrock-Access`) with an assume role policy allowing EC2 instances to assume it. It then attaches the `AmazonBedrockFullAccess` managed policy to grant necessary Bedrock permissions and creates an instance profile. Finally, it associates the instance profile with a specified EC2 instance.

```bash
# 1. Create IAM role and instance profile
aws iam create-role --role-name EC2-Bedrock-Access \
  --assume-role-policy-document '{
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [{
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Principal": {"Service": "ec2.amazonaws.com"},
      "Action": "sts:AssumeRole"
    }]
  }'

aws iam attach-role-policy --role-name EC2-Bedrock-Access \
  --policy-arn arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonBedrockFullAccess

aws iam create-instance-profile --instance-profile-name EC2-Bedrock-Access
aws iam add-role-to-instance-profile \
  --instance-profile-name EC2-Bedrock-Access \
  --role-name EC2-Bedrock-Access

# 2. Attach to your EC2 instance
aws ec2 associate-iam-instance-profile \
  --instance-id i-xxxxx \
  --iam-instance-profile Name=EC2-Bedrock-Access

# 3. On the EC2 instance, enable discovery
openclaw config set models.bedrockDiscovery.enabled true
openclaw config set models.bedrockDiscovery.region us-east-1

# 4. Set the workaround env vars
echo 'export AWS_PROFILE=default' >> ~/.bashrc
echo 'export AWS_REGION=us-east-1' >> ~/.bashrc
source ~/.bashrc

# 5. Verify models are discovered
openclaw models list
```

--------------------------------

### Set AWS Credentials for Bedrock (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/bedrock.md

These bash commands set environment variables for AWS authentication, which are used by the AWS SDK for accessing Amazon Bedrock. This includes access keys, secret access keys, region, and optionally session tokens or a Bedrock bearer token. These are typically set on the gateway host where OpenClaw is running.

```bash
export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID="AKIA..."
export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY="..."
export AWS_REGION="us-east-1"
# Optional:
export AWS_SESSION_TOKEN="..."
export AWS_PROFILE="your-profile"
# Optional (Bedrock API key/bearer token):
export AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK="..."
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Bedrock Automatic Model Discovery (JSON5)

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/bedrock.md

This JSON5 configuration enables automatic discovery of streaming and text-output capable Bedrock models. It allows filtering by provider, setting a refresh interval for cached model lists, and defining default context window and max tokens for discovered models. The `enabled` field defaults to true if AWS credentials are found.

```json5
{
  models: {
    bedrockDiscovery: {
      enabled: true,
      region: "us-east-1",
      providerFilter: ["anthropic", "amazon"],
      refreshInterval: 3600,
      defaultContextWindow: 32000,
      defaultMaxTokens: 4096
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### EC2 Instance Role Workaround for OpenClaw (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/bedrock.md

This bash snippet provides a workaround for OpenClaw's credential detection when using EC2 instance roles for AWS authentication. By setting `AWS_PROFILE=default` and `AWS_REGION`, you signal to the AWS SDK that credentials are available, even though OpenClaw's direct check might miss IMDS credentials. The actual authentication still relies on the attached IAM role.

```bash
# Add to ~/.bashrc or your shell profile
export AWS_PROFILE=default
export AWS_REGION=us-east-1
```

--------------------------------

### Manually Configure Bedrock Provider and Model (JSON5)

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/bedrock.md

This JSON5 configuration manually defines an Amazon Bedrock provider and a specific model. It specifies the base URL for the Bedrock runtime, the API type (`bedrock-converse-stream`), authentication method (`aws-sdk`), and details for the model including its ID, name, supported input types, cost, context window, and max tokens. This configuration is added to OpenClaw's settings.

```json5
{
  models: {
    providers: {
      "amazon-bedrock": {
        baseUrl: "https://bedrock-runtime.us-east-1.amazonaws.com",
        api: "bedrock-converse-stream",
        auth: "aws-sdk",
        models: [
          {
            id: "anthropic.claude-opus-4-5-20251101-v1:0",
            name: "Claude Opus 4.5 (Bedrock)",
            reasoning: true,
            input: ["text", "image"],
            cost: { input: 0, output: 0, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0 },
            contextWindow: 200000,
            maxTokens: 8192
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  },
  agents: {
    defaults: {
      model: { primary: "amazon-bedrock/anthropic.claude-opus-4-5-20251101-v1:0" }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install and Configure Tailscale on VPS

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/help/faq.md

Installs Tailscale on a Virtual Private Server (VPS) using a script and enables it for use. This allows the VPS to join a Tailscale network for secure access.

```bash
curl -fsSL https://tailscale.com/install.sh | sh
sudo tailscale up
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Canvas File Server (JSON)

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/gateway/configuration.md

Sets up the canvasHost component, which serves HTML/CSS/JS files over HTTP with live reload capabilities. It allows iOS/Android nodes to navigate to served content. Configuration includes the root directory, port, and live reload behavior.

```json
{
  "canvasHost": {
    "root": "~/.openclaw/workspace/canvas",
    "port": 18793,
    "liveReload": true
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Fix npm Global Install Permissions (Linux)

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/install/node.md

Resolves 'EACCES' errors during global npm installations on Linux by changing npm's default global prefix to a user-owned directory and updating the PATH accordingly. This avoids the need for sudo.

```bash
mkdir -p "$HOME/.npm-global"
npm config set prefix "$HOME/.npm-global"
export PATH="$HOME/.npm-global/bin:$PATH"
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Cache TTL and Heartbeat for Warm Cache

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/token-use.md

This YAML configuration snippet demonstrates how to set a cache control TTL of 1 hour and a heartbeat interval of 55 minutes for the 'anthropic/claude-opus-4-5' model. This setup aims to keep the cache warm and reduce re-caching costs during idle periods.

```yaml
agents:
  defaults:
    model:
      primary: "anthropic/claude-opus-4-5"
    models:
      "anthropic/claude-opus-4-5":
        params:
          cacheControlTtl: "1h"
    heartbeat:
      every: "55m"
```

--------------------------------

### Expose Dashboard with Tailscale Serve (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/channels/googlechat.md

This command uses Tailscale Serve to expose the OpenClaw dashboard (port 8443) to the tailnet. It supports binding to localhost or a specific Tailscale IP address.

```bash
# If bound to localhost (127.0.0.1 or 0.0.0.0):
tailscale serve --bg --https 8443 http://127.0.0.1:18789

# If bound to Tailscale IP only (e.g., 100.106.161.80):
tailscale serve --bg --https 8443 http://100.106.161.80:18789
```

--------------------------------

### Per-Channel vs Per-Account Visibility Examples (YAML)

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/gateway/heartbeat.md

This YAML snippet provides examples of configuring heartbeat visibility settings at different scopes: channel defaults, specific channels, and individual accounts within a channel. It demonstrates how to customize message delivery behavior.

```yaml
channels:
  defaults:
    heartbeat:
      showOk: false
      showAlerts: true
      useIndicator: true
  slack:
    heartbeat:
      showOk: true # all Slack accounts
    accounts:
      ops:
        heartbeat:
          showAlerts: false # suppress alerts for the ops account only
  telegram:
    heartbeat:
      showOk: true
```

--------------------------------

### Connect via SSH to DigitalOcean Droplet

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/platforms/digitalocean.md

Establishes an SSH connection to the newly created DigitalOcean droplet using the root user and the droplet's IP address. This is a prerequisite for further configuration.

```bash
ssh root@YOUR_DROPLET_IP
```

--------------------------------

### Use OpenClaw Common Sandbox Image

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/install/docker.md

This JSON configuration snippet specifies the use of the `openclaw-sandbox-common:bookworm-slim` Docker image for the agent's sandbox environment. This provides a pre-configured environment with common development tools.

```json5
{
  agents: { defaults: { sandbox: { docker: { image: "openclaw-sandbox-common:bookworm-slim" } } } }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Render Blueprint Configuration (YAML)

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/render.mdx

Defines the OpenClaw service on Render, including runtime, environment variables, and persistent disk storage. This YAML file configures the web service type, Docker runtime, health check path, environment variables such as PORT and SETUP_PASSWORD, and a persistent disk mounted at /data.

```yaml
services:
  - type: web
    name: openclaw
    runtime: docker
    plan: starter
    healthCheckPath: /health
    envVars:
      - key: PORT
        value: "8080"
      - key: SETUP_PASSWORD
        sync: false          # prompts during deploy
      - key: OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR
        value: /data/.openclaw
      - key: OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR
        value: /data/workspace
      - key: OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN
        generateValue: true  # auto-generates a secure token
    disk:
      name: openclaw-data
      mountPath: /data
      sizeGB: 1
```

--------------------------------

### Access OpenClaw Dashboard via SSH Tunnel

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/platforms/digitalocean.md

Sets up an SSH tunnel to securely access the OpenClaw dashboard running on the droplet from your local machine. This is the recommended method for accessing the UI.

```bash
# From your local machine
ssh -L 18789:localhost:18789 root@YOUR_DROPLET_IP

# Then open: http://localhost:18789
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Tailscale Funnel for Public Gateway Access (JSON)

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/gateway/tailscale.md

This configuration enables Tailscale Funnel for public HTTPS access to the Gateway dashboard. It binds the Gateway to loopback and requires a shared password for authentication. Tailscale v1.38.3+, MagicDNS, and HTTPS enabled are prerequisites.

```json
{
  "gateway": {
    "bind": "loopback",
    "tailscale": { "mode": "funnel" },
    "auth": { "mode": "password", "password": "replace-me" }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure OpenClaw Gateway for Tailscale Serve

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/platforms/digitalocean.md

Configures the OpenClaw gateway to use Tailscale Serve for secure, authenticated access to the dashboard. This involves installing Tailscale, enabling it, and setting the gateway's bind mode. It also outlines how to adjust authentication settings.

```bash
# On the droplet
curl -fsSL https://tailscale.com/install.sh | sh
tailscale up

# Configure Gateway to use Tailscale Serve
openclaw config set gateway.tailscale.mode serve
openclaw gateway restart
```

--------------------------------

### Disable Edge TTS Configuration (JSON5)

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/tts.md

This configuration snippet shows how to explicitly disable the Edge TTS provider, even if TTS is otherwise enabled. This is useful if you only want to use paid services like OpenAI or ElevenLabs.

```json5
{
  messages: {
    tts: {
      edge: {
        enabled: false
      }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Heartbeat Visibility Controls (YAML)

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/gateway/heartbeat.md

This configuration snippet illustrates how to control the visibility of heartbeat acknowledgments (`HEARTBEAT_OK`) and alert messages across different channels and accounts. It shows precedence rules for these settings.

```yaml
channels:
  defaults:
    heartbeat:
      showOk: false      # Hide HEARTBEAT_OK (default)
      showAlerts: true   # Show alert messages (default)
      useIndicator: true # Emit indicator events (default)
  telegram:
    heartbeat:
      showOk: true       # Show OK acknowledgments on Telegram
  whatsapp:
    accounts:
      work:
        heartbeat:
          showAlerts: false # Suppress alert delivery for this account
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Legacy TCP Bridge (JSON)

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/gateway/configuration.md

Defines settings for the legacy TCP bridge, which is now removed but historically allowed nodes to connect via TCP. This section covers enabling/disabling the bridge, port, bind address, and TLS settings. TLS configuration includes enabling, auto-generation of certificates, and paths for certificate and key files.

```json
{
  "bridge": {
    "enabled": true,
    "port": 18790,
    "bind": "tailnet",
    "tls": {
      "enabled": true,
      "certPath": "~/.openclaw/bridge/tls/bridge-cert.pem",
      "keyPath": "~/.openclaw/bridge/tls/bridge-key.pem"
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure OpenClaw Heartbeat Settings

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/help/faq.md

JSON configuration to adjust the heartbeat interval for agents. This allows tuning how often heartbeats occur or disabling them entirely by setting the 'every' value to '0m'.

```json5
{
  agents: {
    defaults: {
      heartbeat: {
        every: "2h"   // or "0m" to disable
      }
    }
  }
}

```
